weekly_brief_narrative: Search results yield no confirmed lead escalation signal across eight theatres, with primary data limited to institutional references and OSINT methodologies rather than specific events from April 15-22, 2026. Iran maintains the most visible monitoring via ACLED daily crisis coding and Atlantic Council US asset tracking, indicating stable I2 posture without troop surges or new deployments. Russia-Ukraine shows routine OSINT surveillance of aircraft and satellites, but drone operations and rhetoric remain at baseline levels, with no nuclear episodic rhetoric noted post-2024 doctrine shift. Gaza lacks fresh reports, presuming entrenched stalemate. Sudan Civil War benefits from ACLED's Africa focus, confirming data availability amid structural under-coverage, but no displacement accelerations. Myanmar and DRC Eastern Conflict similarly rest on ACLED real-time event repositories, with stable violence trajectories and no territorial shifts. Iran tensions persist without diplomatic breakthroughs, de-weighted US channels notwithstanding. Sahel Arc and Haiti WATCH theatres report null findings. Displacement trends show no velocity spikes, though IDMC gaps persist. Humanitarian corridors unmentioned. Coverage failure flagged: Sudan/Myanmar/DRC yield only 3 combined entries from Tier 1, signaling source access limitations. Monitor thresholds next week include ACLED daily Iran maps for I2 changes, ISW Russia-Ukraine updates for swarm drone doctrine, UN OCHA Sudan flows for I6 acceleration, and Reuters/AP for any ambassador actions in Iran or Sahel.

theatres_with_thin_coverage: Sudan/Myanmar/DRC (fewer than 3 combined; structural Tier 1 gaps)
